name: Alto del Naranco – circular by the Nalón river from Soto Ribera
name_original: Alto del Naranco – circular por el río Nalón desde Soto Ribera
region_intro: Road cycling around Ribera De Arriba offers diverse terrain within the Central Mountains of Asturias, characterized by lush forests, green meadows, and the extensive Nalón River Valley. The region presents a varied landscape, from relatively flat sections along riverbanks to challenging climbs through mountainous areas. Cyclists encounter a blend of natural features and an established network of routes, including parts of the Central Mountain Cycling Ring. This area provides a range of road cycling experiences suitable for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Ribera De Arriba The most popular road cycling route is Alto del Naranco – circular by the Nalón river from Soto Ribera, a 22.9 miles (36.8 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto del Padrún – Alto de La Colladona (850 m) loop from Olloniego, a difficult 51.5 miles (82.8 km) path that includes high mountain passes. Local road cyclists also love the Valdemurrio Reservoir – Valle del Oso loop from Soto Ribera, a 56.2 miles (90.4 km) trail leading through reservoir areas and natural valleys, often completed in about 6 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Ribera De Arriba is defined by the Central Mountains, the Nalón River Valley, and extensive forests, offering both secondary roads and greenways. The network provides options for various ability levels, from riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Ribera De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 12,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ribera De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Ribera De Arriba?
A: Ribera De Arriba offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with nearly &lt;strong&gt;190 tours&lt;/strong&gt; available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Ribera De Arriba?
A: The terrain in Ribera De Arriba is highly diverse, reflecting its location in the Central Mountains of Asturias. You'll find everything from relatively flat sections along the &lt;strong&gt;Nalón River Valley&lt;/strong&gt; to demanding climbs through mountainous areas. The region is characterized by lush forests, green meadows, and offers both secondary roads and greenways for varied riding experiences.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes feature significant elevation, there are options suitable for less experienced cyclists. The region includes greenways like the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Fuso&lt;/strong&gt;, which offer comfortable and safe riding. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077507220/alto-del-naranco-circular-by-the-nalon-river-from-soto-ribera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Naranco – circular by the Nalón river from Soto Ribera&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a scenic ride with manageable challenges.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31860950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Padrún – Alto de La Colladona (850 m) loop from Olloniego&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 51.5-mile path featuring high mountain passes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20506032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemurrio Reservoir – Valle del Oso loop from Soto Ribera&lt;/a&gt;, a 56.2-mile trail with substantial elevation gain.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while cycling in Ribera De Arriba?
A: Ribera De Arriba offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle past significant mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5914390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Padrún&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1034775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of El Angliru&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby village of Bueño/Güeño is famous for its traditional &lt;strong&gt;Hórreos&lt;/strong&gt; (raised granaries) and an Interpretation Centre. The region also features the historic &lt;strong&gt;Route of the Silver (Ruta de la Plata)&lt;/strong&gt;, a Roman route that crosses Ribera de Arriba.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Ribera De Arriba is part of the extensive &lt;strong&gt;Central Mountain Cycling Ring (Anillo Ciclista de la Montaña Central)&lt;/strong&gt;, which connects various municipalities and offers numerous circular route possibilities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077507220/alto-del-naranco-circular-by-the-nalon-river-from-soto-ribera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Naranco – circular by the Nalón river from Soto Ribera&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option, and the Bueño-Las Caldas-El Caleyu route is another circular trail connecting charming towns.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Ribera De Arriba?
A: The road cycling routes in Ribera De Arriba are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;4.5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 180 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty with industrial heritage, and the well-established network of routes that cater to all levels.

Q: Is Ribera De Arriba known for any major cycling events?
A: Yes, Ribera De Arriba has a notable connection to professional cycling. It was a stage host for the &lt;strong&gt;Vuelta a Asturias cycling race in 2017&lt;/strong&gt;, with a route from Ribera De Arriba to Alto del Acebo. This highlights the region's reputation as a significant destination for cycle tourism.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Ribera De Arriba?
A: The region's lush landscapes suggest that spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery for road cycling. Summer can also be excellent, especially in the higher elevations, though riverside paths might be warmer. Winter cycling is possible, but weather conditions can be more variable, with potential for colder temperatures and precipitation in the mountains.

Q: Are there options for public transport or parking near the cycling routes?
A: Ribera De Arriba's proximity to Oviedo, just 7 kilometers away, makes it easily accessible. This suggests good public transport links to the capital, from where you can reach the region. For those arriving by car, parking is generally available in towns and villages, providing convenient access to various trailheads. The local government also promotes sustainable tourism with a free electric bicycle service, making routes more accessible.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other outdoor activities in Ribera De Arriba?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;strong&gt;Nalón River&lt;/strong&gt;, which flows through the municipality, offers opportunities for activities like canoeing and kayaking, with a public jetty available. The region's extensive network of trails is also suitable for hiking and mountain biking (BTT), allowing for a diverse outdoor experience beyond road cycling.
tips: very nice bike path. some sections are no good for roadbikes

Around the middle of the last century, the idea arose to build a figure of Christ on Mount Naranco in Oviedo similar to the one in Rio de Janeiro, but the iconic figure of the Sacred Heart took 18 years to build and was completed in 1980.
